title: Using 'Dar' - To Give
description: Learn how to use the verb 'dar' (to give) in various contexts and pronouns.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: The word for 'to give' in Spanish is dar. D-A-R, dar.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Dar. Good. 'I give' is doy, ending in -oy. Doy.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'I give it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'I give them'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. And if they were feminine things?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'he gives'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Da. To emphasize 'he', you say:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Él da. Good. And 'she gives'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How do you say 'they give'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good! 'Ellos dan' or 'ellas dan' for feminine. How about 'you (informal) give'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Das. To emphasize 'you', you say:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. The word for 'something' is algo. How would you say 'he is giving me something'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good! 'Él me da algo.' How about 'they give me something'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'Me dan algo.' Now, to say 'they give it to me', you say 'me lo dan'. How would you emphasize 'they'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Ellos me lo dan. Good. Or 'ellas' for feminine. Now, if you want to say 'I will give it to you tomorrow', you can just say 'I give it to you tomorrow'. How would you say this informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Te lo doy mañana. Very Spanish sounding. Now, how would you say 'are you giving it to me tomorrow?' formally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. To emphasize 'you', you say:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Usted me lo da mañana? These pronouns are flexible for emphasis. Now, 'you guys' is 'ustedes'. How would you say 'are you guys giving it to us tomorrow?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. To clarify 'you guys', you say:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How do you say 'I want to give'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'I want to give it to you' informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good.

outro.text: You've learned how to use the verb 'dar' with different pronouns and contexts.
Try using 'dar' in a sentence about giving something to someone tomorrow.
Practice these structures to get comfortable with giving in Spanish.
